title: Improve dashboard cards

tasks.title: Replace dashboard lifecycle status with gate progress and sort cards

tasks.implementation_summary: Added structural `GATE_ORDER` and `completedGates` fields for ADV status cards. Each dashboard lane sorts `adv_change_status` items by completed gate count descending, then last activity descending, title ascending, and id ascending; degraded items remain first in `needs_attention`. Removed lifecycle status rendering from ADV status cards and legacy ADV change cards, replacing it with `Gate progress` display alongside the existing next-gate badge. Updated dashboard model/UI/server tests for new sort and rendering behavior.
tasks.notes: Completed with checkpoint 51d9d32b. Dashboard cards now show gate progress instead of lifecycle status, and ADV cards sort by completed gate count descending with deterministic tie-breaks. Unmatched source behavior unchanged.

subagent_reports.topic: Dashboard card gate-badge/progress + intra-lane sort + unmatched_source semantics validation against repo-local architecture

sources.label: bin/lib/dashboard/attention.ts
sources.summary: buildAttentionLanes filters buildChangeStatusItems() per latest.overall into needs_attention/running/ready_landed/backlog; unmatched_source comes from buildUnmatchedSource(input.unlinked) only. changeStatusItem already populates gate=firstIncompleteGate??'complete' and progress=gateProgressStr. NO intra-lane sort exists today; order = input.changes Map insertion. degradedItems are prepended to needs_attention (must stay ahead of sorted ADV cards).

sources.label: bin/lib/dashboard/ui.ts
sources.summary: changeStatusHtml already renders a gate badge via gateClass() with CSS .gate-badge/.gate-acceptance/.gate-release/.gate-complete. BUT it also still renders a 'Status: <status>' block (line 119) — this is the AC1 target to replace. renderLane (172-174) renders items in array order, so sorting must be done upstream in attention.ts, not in UI.

sources.label: bin/lib/dashboard/adv.ts
sources.summary: DashboardAdvChange carries gateProgressStr:string + firstIncompleteGate:string|null + lastActivityAt + id + title, sourced from ChangeSummary. It does NOT carry the raw gate map, so completed-gate count must be derived from gateProgressStr glyphs or from firstIncompleteGate index in GATE_ORDER.

sources.label: bin/lib/changes.ts
sources.summary: Canonical GATE_ORDER = [proposal,discovery,design,planning,execution,acceptance,release]. buildGateProgress maps done->✓ else ○ in GATE_ORDER sequence. firstIncompleteGate returns first non-done gate id or null when all done. Index of firstIncompleteGate in GATE_ORDER == completed-gate count (null => 7). This gives a structural completed-count without glyph parsing.

sources.label: adv_change_show contract (improveDashboardCards)
sources.summary: Approved standard-rigor contract: AC1 replace Status:draft with gate progress/badge; AC2 sort more-complete first; AC3 deterministic tiebreak by recency/title/id; AC4 unmatched stays unmatched_source w/o structural conversion; AC5 targeted tests cover. Constraints: no mutation, no secrets, local read-only, implementation-only (no spec-law change).

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The approach aligns tightly with the existing reframeDashboardStatus architecture (archived 2026-06-26). All required data is already plumbed: gate badge rendering and gate/progress fields exist on ChangeStatusLaneItem (attention.ts changeStatusItem) and in changeStatusHtml. The change is genuinely a thin presentation/model refinement, not new infrastructure. Three concrete deltas: (1) AC1 — remove/replace the residual 'Status: <status>' block in changeStatusHtml (ui.ts:119) with gate progress text; the badge already renders. (2) AC2/AC3 — add a deterministic sort of adv_change_status items inside buildAttentionLanes per-lane after filtering (attention.ts:127-139), keying on completed-gate count descending then a stable tiebreak. (3) AC4 — no code change needed; unmatched_source already derives solely from input.unlinked (source-only) and is never converted to an ADV card. Structural-correctness note (P33): derive completed-gate count from GATE_ORDER.indexOf(firstIncompleteGate) (null => GATE_ORDER.length) rather than parsing ✓ glyphs from gateProgressStr — index lookup is structural and robust to display-string format drift; both attention.ts and ui.ts/render.ts use slightly different gateProgressStr formats (compact '✓ ✓ ...' vs labeled 'proposal ✓ discovery ✓ ...' seen in tests), so glyph-counting is fragile across producers. Sorting must live in attention.ts (model), not ui.ts renderLane, because renderLane preserves array order; and the degradedItems prepend in needs_attention must remain ahead of sorted ADV cards.

validation.notes: VALIDATED. No architectural conflict, no spec-law impact (no dashboard capability law governs card presentation/lane sort; design+proposal spec-law-impact analysis confirmed). Risks are localized: (R1) gateProgressStr format is inconsistent across producers/tests (compact vs labeled) — prefer firstIncompleteGate index for completed-count to avoid brittle glyph parsing. (R2) sort placement: must apply in buildAttentionLanes after per-lane filter, preserving degraded-source prepend order in needs_attention. (R3) AC3 deterministic tiebreak needs a total order — use lastActivityAt desc then title then id to guarantee stability (DashboardAdvChange carries lastActivityAt + id + title). (R4) ChangeStatusLaneItem currently lacks lastActivityAt; sort either needs it threaded onto the item or must sort the DashboardAdvChange/accumulator set before constructing items. (R5) UI tests assert presence of 'Status' text today — removing the Status block will require deliberate test updates (same coupled-contract pattern flagged in prior reframeDashboardStatus archive).

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed (VALIDATED). Implementation guidance: (1) Add a pure completedGateCount(change) helper deriving from GATE_ORDER.indexOf(firstIncompleteGate) (null => 7) — do NOT count ✓ glyphs. (2) Sort within buildAttentionLanes per lane after filtering: completedGateCount desc, then lastActivityAt desc, then title asc, then id asc; keep degradedItems prepended ahead of sorted needs_attention ADV cards. (3) Thread lastActivityAt onto ChangeStatusLaneItem (or sort accumulators/changes before item construction) so the tiebreak data is available. (4) In changeStatusHtml replace the 'Status: <status>' block with a gate-progress line; retain the existing gate badge. (5) Leave buildUnmatchedSource untouched for AC4. Test focus: model test for completedGateCount edge cases (all ○, all ✓/null, mixed); intra-lane sort ordering test with differing gate progress (AC2) and equal-progress deterministic tiebreak (AC3); UI test asserting 'Status: draft' no longer renders and gate progress does (AC1); regression test that unmatched GitHub source stays in unmatched_source and is not converted to adv_change_status (AC4); verify degraded source still leads needs_attention after sort; run via 'bin/oc-test targeted -- bin/lib/dashboard' then full 'bun test bin/'.
follow_ups: TASK_SCOPE/IN_SCOPE/OUT_OF_SCOPE/DONE_WHEN/STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION packet anchors were not provided in the prompt; proceeded with prompt scope per policy.

Confirm whether gateProgressStr format divergence (compact vs labeled, seen in attention.test.ts:174 vs adv.test.ts:25) is intentional; if a single producer feeds the dashboard it is moot, but it reinforces using firstIncompleteGate index over glyph parsing.

documents.proposal:
# Improve dashboard cards

## Intent
Replace repeated `Status: draft` on ADV dashboard cards with meaningful gate progress, and order cards inside each dashboard column from most-complete gate progress to least-complete.

## LBP Targets
- Use existing gate data already present in dashboard change models; do not infer state from Temporal lifecycle `status`.
- Sort ADV-centered cards by deterministic gate progress descending, with stable secondary ordering.
- Preserve read-only local dashboard behavior and unmatched-source semantics.

## Scope
- `bin/lib/dashboard/attention.ts`
- `bin/lib/dashboard/ui.ts`
- Related tests under `bin/lib/dashboard/*test.ts`

## User Outcomes
- ADV cards no longer display noisy `Status: draft` for in-progress changes.
- Cards show a gate badge/progress signal users can act on.
- Columns prioritize more-complete changes first.
- Unmatched source remains secondary and still means GitHub source activity with no structural ADV match.

## Spec-law impact
No spec law update required: existing specs do not define the local dashboard card presentation/lane sorting behavior, and this change preserves read-only dashboard constraints, safe rendering, and unmatched-source semantics.
documents.problemStatement: Dashboard ADV cards currently show `Status: draft`, which is not useful because many in-progress ADV changes retain draft lifecycle status due to Temporal behavior. Columns also do not prioritize most-complete work first.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives
1. Replace `Status: draft` card text with a gate badge/progress signal derived from ADV gate data.
2. Sort ADV cards inside each column from most-complete gate progress to least-complete.
3. Preserve read-only dashboard behavior, safe rendering, and unmatched-source semantics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given an ADV card has gate data, when rendered, then it shows gate progress/badge text instead of `Status: draft`.
- Given two ADV cards in the same column have different gate progress, when rendered, then the card with more completed gates appears first.
- Given two ADV cards have equal gate progress, when rendered, then ordering remains deterministic by recency/title/id.
- Given unmatched GitHub source exists, when rendered, then it remains in `unmatched_source` and is not converted to an ADV card without structural match.
- Given dashboard tests run, when targeted dashboard tests complete, then card rendering and sorting behavior are covered.

## Constraints
- No mutation controls.
- No token/secret display.
- Local-only, read-only dashboard.
- Implementation-only presentation/model refinement; no dashboard capability law change expected.
documents.design:
# Design

## Approach
- Compute gate completion score from existing ADV gate fields on dashboard change records.
- Replace lifecycle status rendering in `changeStatusHtml`/card template with a gate progress badge and next-gate badge.
- Sort `adv_change_status` cards within each lane by completed gate count descending, then deterministic fallback ordering.
- Leave source-only items in `unmatched_source` unchanged.

## Spec-law impact
No spec law update required: `adv_spec list` and targeted search for dashboard status/card/gate/sort/unmatched-source requirements found no dashboard capability law that needs modification. This is an implementation-only refinement of local CLI dashboard presentation.

## Validation
- Add/update model tests for lane/card ordering.
- Add/update UI tests asserting `Status: draft` no longer renders for ADV cards and gate progress does.
- Run targeted dashboard tests, then restart service and verify `/` and `/api/state`.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

Updated the local ADV dashboard cards so in-progress changes no longer show noisy `Status: draft` lifecycle text. ADV status cards now show the existing next-gate badge plus `Gate progress`, derived from already-plumbed gate data.

Cards inside each lane now sort by completed gate count descending, then by last activity, title, and change id for deterministic ordering. This puts the most-complete changes first without relying on Temporal lifecycle status.

Unmatched source semantics remain unchanged: GitHub PR/workflow/deployment items without structural ADV correlation stay in the secondary `unmatched_source` lane, which covers one-off or small-fix source activity that has no ADV change match.

Verification: RED `tr_mqv90qsc_23a4c171`; GREEN `tr_mqv9321x_efe255b8`; dashboard suite `tr_mqv9668v_e9fc46a2` (41 tests, 192 assertions); reviewer suite `tr_mqv9at47_0137ffb4` (22 tests, 124 assertions); reviewer verdict READY.
